hRTe7tSkR2k.desc: Bookmark at 44:03  If you have a constant column in machine learning, that means there's no variance in column B which explains variance in column C, which directly means that there is nothing that can explain the variation in C column. And so you directly drop that column.

Bookmark at 46:12  If you receive constant columns or quasi constant columns in your dataset, the way to remove them is using variance threshold. There are three steps in variance threshold. First step is to decide a threshold value, which is let's say 0.1. In the second step, you calculate variance of all the columns individually. And in the third step, whichever columns have variance less than the threshold value, you simply drop them.

Bookmark at 47:29  What should happen in this case? There are a lot of questions, but I will give you a simple guideline. Generally, when you put a variance threshold, make sure that you have to normalize your columns. Take the same scale. Because if the scales are different, the same threshold value will not help. So a good strategy would be to see your data. In this case, the data is normalized. If data is normalized, then it is said that between 0.01 to 0.01 threshold. What did I do? I did not get more brainwashed and put threshold value as 0.5

Bookmark at 57:58 s We are talking about Pearson correlation coefficient. Although you can use Spearman and kendall, we will use Pearson correlation coefficient. You will know that you will take two columns with this. This is your input column and this is your output column. If it is minus 1, it means strong inverse linear relationship. If it is plus 1, it means strong positive linear relationship. If relationship is point 9, then it means strong positive linear relationship. correlation is equal to 0, no linear relationship is equal to 0. Non-linear relationship is equal to 0. I hope you know how co-relation coefficient works in statistics.
